name: Réveillon (Rio New Year)
traditions: Massive fireworks over Copacabana beach; wearing white for peace; offering flowers to sea goddess Iemanjá; jumping seven waves; live music.
historicalBackground: Rio's Réveillon blends Catholic and Afro-Brazilian (Candomblé) traditions. Offerings to Iemanjá reflect the syncretism of Yoruba and Catholic beliefs.
description: The world's largest New Year's Eve beach party, with millions gathering on Copacabana for fireworks, music, and Afro-Brazilian sea offerings.
